data.title: BitGo: Core Institutional Crypto Infrastructure Benefiting From Regulated DeFi and Tokenized Assets Growth
data.description:
BitGo Holdings, Inc. Class A (BTGO) has received a new Buy rating, initiated by Citi analyst, Peter Christiansen.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Peter Christiansen has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to BitGo’s strategic positioning in institutional crypto infrastructure. He views the company as a core hub for on-chain finance, evolving from a pure custody provider into a broader workflow and infrastructure platform that can capture a larger share of client spending as digital assets mature.

Christiansen also argues that BitGo is well placed to benefit from the growth of regulated DeFi and tokenized real‑world assets, supported by its security-focused, multi‑signature custody and strong compliance framework. Despite trimming near‑term revenue and EBITDA estimates to reflect recent crypto volatility, he believes the company’s projected high-teens margins and roughly 30% medium‑term revenue growth justify the $18 target price and Buy/High Risk rating.

Christiansen covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Strategy, Bullish, and Pagaya Technologies Ltd. According to TipRanks, Christiansen has an average return of -0.2% and a 49.53% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: ITM Power: Raised FY26 Guidance and Growing Backlog Underpin Buy Rating Despite Green Hydrogen Volatility
data.description:
ITM Power, the Industrials sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Constantin Hesse from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a p115.00 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Constantin Hesse has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to ITM Power’s improving commercial profile and financial guidance. The company has lifted its FY26 revenue outlook by about 11%, reflecting faster-than-expected project execution and the early benefits from newly signed contracts, signaling stronger demand for its electrolyzer solutions despite sector-wide uncertainty.

He also points to ITM Power’s expanding contract backlog, which has risen markedly over the past two years, indicating growing visibility on future revenues. In Hesse’s view, near-term volatility in the green hydrogen market is mitigated by the company’s robust cash position and unchanged EBITDA guidance, supporting the investment case and justifying a positive stance on the shares.

data.title: InterContinental Hotels: RevPAR Acceleration, Margin Expansion, and Buy-Rated EPS Growth Outpacing Medium-Term Targets
data.description:
InterContinental Hotels, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Simon LeChipre from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $160.00 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Simon LeChipre’s rating is based on InterContinental Hotels delivering broadly strong full‑year 2025 results, with a notable uplift in fee margins supported by operating leverage and higher ancillary fees, translating into adjusted EPS growth already running ahead of the company’s medium‑term trajectory. He also highlights the improving momentum in fourth‑quarter RevPAR, which underpins his conviction that year‑on‑year RevPAR gains will accelerate into 2026.

He expects this RevPAR acceleration, when combined with continued net system size expansion, further fee margin improvement, and ongoing share repurchases, to support another year of low‑ to mid‑teens EPS growth. In his view, this prospective earnings profile is competitive with leading U.S. hotel peers, reinforcing the attractiveness of the stock at current levels and justifying his Buy recommendation.

In another report released on February 2, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $165.00 price target.
